summary: Frontend testing completed for homepage H1/H2 duplicate removal and GEO/Twitter meta tag additions. All 3 tasks passed successfully across desktop (1920px), mobile (375px), and tablet (800px) viewports.

tests.value: Appliance Repair in San Francisco — Fixed Same Day

tests.test: H1 visible on mobile (375px) with smaller font
Desktop CTAs visible on desktop, hidden on mobile
Mobile CTAs visible on mobile, hidden on desktop
Desktop logo visible on desktop, hidden on mobile
'Where We Serve' appears exactly once (after lazy load)
No duplicate H2s on any viewport
meta[name='twitter:title'] = 'Appliance Repair SF Bay Area | FixitBay'
meta[name='twitter:description'] = 'Same-day appliance repair in San Francisco. $60 diagnostic, 180-day warranty, licensed CA technician.'
preconnect for fonts.gstatic.com with crossorigin exists
How It Works section visible on desktop and mobile

context_for_next_testing_agent: Homepage H1/H2 duplicate removal and GEO/Twitter meta tag tasks verified complete. Hero uses CSS classes (hero-d, hero-d-flex, hero-m) for desktop/mobile show/hide. Lazy-loaded ServiceAreaMapLibre component loads 'Where We Serve' H2 after scroll. All meta tags set correctly via SEOMetaTags.js component and index.html inline script.
